issues.title: Handling Illegal `Accept-Events` Header
issues.author: CxRes
issues.body:
Where a recipient receives an illegal `Accept-Events` header field, can we:

+ Ignore it (as we do now)?
+ Send an error message. If so what could be the form of the error message?

issues.title: Protocol identifier as a URL?
issues.author: CxRes
issues.body:
Can we a URL (in lieu of an identifier) to identify the notifications protocol?

If we use such a URL, can we obviate the need for an IANA registry for notifications protocol?

issues.title: Handling response with notifications when not requested
issues.author: CxRes
issues.body:
Does compliance with HTTP demand that we ignore the `Events` header when sent in response to a request that does not contain an `Accept-Events` header? 

How should the recipient process such a response, that presumably contains notifications that it has not requested?

issues.title: Handling `Events` header for unsupported methods
issues.author: CxRes
issues.body:
Does the handling of  `Events` field need to be made more specific for unsupported/unrecognized methods?

Such as:
"A recipient MUST ignore the `Events` header field received in response to a request method that is unrecognized or for which notifications response is not defined for a particular notifications protocol."
